text: The surgeon has robotically mobilized the small bowel, moving it out of the pelvic region, preparing for further steps in the colectomy procedure.
The surgeon has prepared the rectum and mobilized it, followed by dissecting and freeing the efferent limb of the descending colon, which is visibly exiting through the abdominal wall.
The surgeon uses robotic scissors to sharply amputate a section of tissue, demonstrating precise cutting technique.
The surgeon uses the Firefly imaging system to assess perfusion in the surgical field.
The surgeon positions the instrument at the inferior border, just below the skin, within the peristomal hernia area, preparing to make a precise incision.
The surgical assistant places the anvil into the colostomy site, preparing for the next step in the procedure.
The surgeon uses the VesselSealer Xtend to grasp needles and run a running purse-string suture, with a suture already placed in the surgical field.
The surgeon switches to a needle driver for improved needle handling, as the VesselSealer was not optimal for this task.
The surgeon performs a running purse-string suture, threading the suture material continuously around the targeted area.

The surgeon completes the running purse string suture around the anvil, which has been previously dropped into the abdomen. The anvil is then placed intraluminally, and the suture is cinched down to secure it in position.
The surgeon exposes the distal rectum, having dissected and prepared it for the next step. The rectum is then sharply amputated using surgical instruments.
The EEA stapler is positioned transanally, upstream from the proctotomy site, following the sizing and dilation of the rectum.
The surgeon begins running a purse string suture using a second V-lock suture, threading it from outside to inside and then inside to outside around the rectal stump.
The anvil spike is fully extruded from the lumen of the rectum, aiding in the surgical process.
The surgeon completes the purse string suture around the rectal lumen, preparing for anastomosis with the EEA stapler positioned transanally.
The surgeon cinches down the purse string suture and cuts it, completing the closure around the rectal lumen.
The surgeon advances the taper further, successfully snapping it into place, resulting in the retrieval of two robust and healthy anastomotic doughnuts.
